name: Buttermilk Pound Cake (Ew)
recipeIngredient: 2 cans (16 oz each) pears in light syrup, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor or blender, puree the pears.
2. Transfer to a medium-sized saucepan and cook over medium-low heat, stirring almost constantly, until reduced to 1 cup (10 to 15 minutes). Adjust heat as necessary to maintain a gentle simmer. Transfer to a large bowl and let cool completely.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly oil a 10-inch tube pan or coat with nonstick cooking spray. Dust with flour, shaking out excess.
4. In a small saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Cook, swirling the pan, until the butter turns a light, nutty brown (about 60 seconds). Pour into a small bowl and stir in oil.
5. Sift cake flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda into a medium-sized bowl. Add 1 1/2 cups sugar, buttermilk, vanilla, lemon zest, egg yolks, and butter-oil mixture to the reserved pear puree and whisk until smooth.
6. In a clean mixing bowl with clean beaters, beat the 4 egg whites until soft peaks form. While continuing to beat, slowly add the remaining 1/4 cup sugar and beat until stiff but not dry peaks form.
7. With a rubber spatula, gently fold the dry ingredients into the pear puree mixture alternately with the beaten whites (3 additions of dry ingredients and 2 additions of beaten whites).
8. Turn batter into prepared pan. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted in center comes out clean. Let cool in pan for 5 minutes, then turn out onto a rack to cool right side up.
